name: Creole Omelet
recipeIngredient: 12 oz Potatoes, peeled and thinly sliced, about 2 cups
1 medium Red bell pepper, cored, seeded and cut into thin strips, about 1 cup
1 medium Onion, diced, about ¾ cup
1 medium Celery, cut into thin 2-inch (4 oz) pieces
½ tsp Ground red cayenne pepper
4 oz Cooked ham, diced, about 1 cup
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 12-inch skillet with heat-proof handle, heat oil over medium-high heat.
2. Add potatoes, red bell pepper, onion, and celery to the skillet. Cook for about 12 minutes, stirring frequently until vegetables are tender.
3. Meanwhile, in a medium bowl, beat eggs, ⅓ cup water, salt, and ground red pepper until well blended.
4. Pour the egg mixture into the skillet and scatter okra and diced ham over the top.
5. Reduce heat to medium. Cook for 10 minutes until the sides and bottom of the omelet are set.
6. Meanwhile, preheat the broiler.
7. Place the skillet under the broiler, about 6 inches from the heat source. Broil for 3 to 5 minutes until the top of the omelet is puffy and just set.
8. Garnish with parsley if desired. Makes 6 servings.
